agent.system_template:
You are a software engineering agent editing a small game bot through a shell.

Issue one bash command at a time. Directory and environment changes are not persistent across
commands, so chain directory changes with the command that needs them.

When the work is complete, submit by running exactly:
`echo COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT`
on its own. After submitting, you cannot continue working this round.
agent.instance_template:
## Game Description

{{game_description}}

## Workspace

The game codebase is in {{working_dir}}.

Useful paths:
- `docs/`: game documentation
- `/logs/`: prior edit and gameplay logs
- `README_agent.md`: handoff notes for later teammates

Improve the bot if you can do so safely within the command budget. Prefer a small, reversible,
well-scoped edit over broad rewrites. If you cannot identify a safe edit, leave a concise note in
`README_agent.md` and submit.

Store final task changes inside {{working_dir}} or subdirectories.

Workspace hygiene control:
- Use `/tmp` for scratch scripts, or delete scratch scripts before final submission.
- Before submitting, inspect the submitted workspace with `git status --short` or equivalent.
- Final submitted diff may include task-relevant source changes and `README_agent.md`.
- Final submitted diff must not include unreferenced helper, scratch, cache, generated, or
  secret-risk files.
- Before final submission, run `git diff --check` or an equivalent trailing-whitespace check.
- If that check reports whitespace errors, fix only those whitespace errors before submitting.

Opponent collision control:
- Implement a narrow safety layer in `main.py`.
- Preserve or add board-boundary checks so moves off the board are unsafe.
- Preserve or add self-collision checks so moves into your own body are unsafe.
- Add opponent-body collision checks so moves into another snake body segment are unsafe.
- Keep the edit source-evident and avoid broad strategy rewrites.
- If you cannot identify a safe opponent-collision edit, write that null decision in
  `README_agent.md` and submit a clean workspace.

Final inspection control:
- After all edits, after any whitespace fixes, and immediately before final submission, run
  exactly `git status --short && git diff --check` or an equivalent final inspection command.
- Do not submit until that final inspection command has returned.
- It is acceptable for `git status --short` to show task files such as `main.py` and
  `README_agent.md`; `git diff --check` must report no errors.
- Do not combine the final inspection command with the submit command.

The round has a limit of {{step_limit}} commands. Submit before the limit.

messages.content:
You are a software engineering agent editing a small game bot through a shell.

Issue one bash command at a time. Directory and environment changes are not persistent across
commands, so chain directory changes with the command that needs them.

When the work is complete, submit by running exactly:
`echo COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT`
on its own. After submitting, you cannot continue working this round.

## Game Description

You are competing in a small BattleSnake coding game.

Your bot controls a snake on an 11x11 board. Snakes collect food, avoid collisions, and try to
outlast opponents. This is a one-round provider-model smoke test. The goal is not a leaderboard
score; the goal is to make a bounded, evidence-capturable agent attempt.

Implement the next narrow safety layer in `main.py`: board-boundary checks, self-collision
checks, and opponent-body collision checks. Document the bounded edit briefly in
`README_agent.md`. If you cannot implement it safely within the command budget, write a short
null note in `README_agent.md` and submit a clean workspace.

## Workspace

The game codebase is in /workspace.

Useful paths:
- `docs/`: game documentation
- `/logs/`: prior edit and gameplay logs
- `README_agent.md`: handoff notes for later teammates

Improve the bot if you can do so safely within the command budget. Prefer a small, reversible,
well-scoped edit over broad rewrites. If you cannot identify a safe edit, leave a concise note in
`README_agent.md` and submit.

Store final task changes inside /workspace or subdirectories.

Workspace hygiene control:
- Use `/tmp` for scratch scripts, or delete scratch scripts before final submission.
- Before submitting, inspect the submitted workspace with `git status --short` or equivalent.
- Final submitted diff may include task-relevant source changes and `README_agent.md`.
- Final submitted diff must not include unreferenced helper, scratch, cache, generated, or
  secret-risk files.
- Before final submission, run `git diff --check` or an equivalent trailing-whitespace check.
- If that check reports whitespace errors, fix only those whitespace errors before submitting.

Opponent collision control:
- Implement a narrow safety layer in `main.py`.
- Preserve or add board-boundary checks so moves off the board are unsafe.
- Preserve or add self-collision checks so moves into your own body are unsafe.
- Add opponent-body collision checks so moves into another snake body segment are unsafe.
- Keep the edit source-evident and avoid broad strategy rewrites.
- If you cannot identify a safe opponent-collision edit, write that null decision in
  `README_agent.md` and submit a clean workspace.

Final inspection control:
- After all edits, after any whitespace fixes, and immediately before final submission, run
  exactly `git status --short && git diff --check` or an equivalent final inspection command.
- Do not submit until that final inspection command has returned.
- It is acceptable for `git status --short` to show task files such as `main.py` and
  `README_agent.md`; `git diff --check` must report no errors.
- Do not combine the final inspection command with the submit command.

The round has a limit of 8 commands. Submit before the limit.

<output>
# Welcome to
# __________         __    __  .__                               __
# \______   \_____ _/  |__/  |_|  |   ____   ______ ____ _____  |  | __ ____
#  |    |  _/\__  \\   __\   __\  | _/ __ \ /  ___//    \\__  \ |  |/ // __ \
#  |    |   \ / __ \|  |  |  | |  |_\  ___/ \___ \|   |  \/ __ \|    <\  ___/
#  |________/(______/__|  |__| |____/\_____>______>___|__(______/__|__\\_____>
#
# This file can be a nice home for your Battlesnake logic and helper functions.
#
# To get you started we've included code to prevent your Battlesnake from moving backwards.
# For more info see docs.battlesnake.com

import random
import typing


# info is called when you create your Battlesnake on play.battlesnake.com
# and controls your Battlesnake's appearance
# TIP: If you open your Battlesnake URL in a browser you should see this data
def info() -> typing.Dict:
    print("INFO")

    return {
        "apiversion": "1",
        "author": "",  # TODO: Your Battlesnake Username
        "color": "#888888",  # TODO: Choose color
        "head": "default",  # TODO: Choose head
        "tail": "default",  # TODO: Choose tail
    }


# start is called when your Battlesnake begins a game
def start(game_state: typing.Dict):
    print("GAME START")


# end is called when your Battlesnake finishes a game
def end(game_state: typing.Dict):
    print("GAME OVER\n")


# move is called on every turn and returns your next move
# Valid moves are "up", "down", "left", or "right"
# See https://docs.battlesnake.com/api/example-move for available data
def move(game_state: typing.Dict) -> typing.Dict:

    is_move_safe = {"up": True, "down": True, "left": True, "right": True}

    # We've included code to prevent your Battlesnake from moving backwards
    my_head = game_state["you"]["body"][0]  # Coordinates of your head
    my_neck = game_state["you"]["body"][1]  # Coordinates of your "neck"

    if my_neck["x"] < my_head["x"]:  # Neck is left of head, don't move left
        is_move_safe["left"] = False

    elif my_neck["x"] > my_head["x"]:  # Neck is right of head, don't move right
        is_move_safe["right"] = False

    elif my_neck["y"] < my_head["y"]:  # Neck is below head, don't move down
        is_move_safe["down"] = False

    elif my_neck["y"] > my_head["y"]:  # Neck is above head, don't move up
        is_move_safe["up"] = False

    # TODO: Step 1 - Prevent your Battlesnake from moving out of bounds
    # board_width = game_state['board']['width']
    # board_height = game_state['board']['height']

    # TODO: Step 2 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with itself
    # my_body = game_state['you']['body']

    # TODO: Step 3 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with other Battlesnakes
    # opponents = game_state['board']['snakes']

    # Are there any safe moves left?
    safe_moves = []
    for move, isSafe in is_move_safe.items():
        if isSafe:
            safe_moves.append(move)

    if len(safe_moves) == 0:
        print(f"MOVE {game_state['turn']}: No safe moves detected! Moving down")
        return {"move": "down"}

    # Choose a random move from the safe ones
    next_move = random.choice(safe_moves)

    # TODO: Step 4 - Move towards food instead of random, to regain health and survive longer
    # food = game_state['board']['food']

    print(f"MOVE {game_state['turn']}: {next_move}")
    return {"move": next_move}


# Start server when `python main.py` is run
if __name__ == "__main__":
    from server import run_server

    run_server({"info": info, "start": start, "move": move, "end": end})
</output>

function.arguments:
{"command": "cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/edit.py
import re

with open('/workspace/main.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

new_code = """
    board_width = game_state['board']['width']
    board_height = game_state['board']['height']
    my_body = game_state['you']['body']
    opponents = game_state['board']['snakes']

    possible_moves = {
        "up": {"x": my_head["x"], "y": my_head["y"] + 1},
        "down": {"x": my_head["x"], "y": my_head["y"] - 1},
        "left": {"x": my_head["x"] - 1, "y": my_head["y"]},
        "right": {"x": my_head["x"] + 1, "y": my_head["y"]}
    }

    for move, next_pos in possible_moves.items():
        if not is_move_safe[move]:
            continue
        
        # Step 1 - Prevent your Battlesnake from moving out of bounds
        if next_pos["x"] < 0 or next_pos["x"] >= board_width or next_pos["y"] < 0 or next_pos["y"] >= board_height:
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue

        # Step 2 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with itself
        if next_pos in my_body[:-1]: # Tail might move, but to be safe we can just check all or all but last. The prompt says "moves into your own body are unsafe".
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue

        # Step 3 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with other Battlesnakes
        collision = False
        for snake in opponents:
            if next_pos in snake['body'][:-1]:
                collision = True
                break
        if collision:
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue
"""

# Replace the TODOs with the new code
content = re.sub(r"    # TODO: Step 1 - Prevent your Battlesnake from moving out of bounds.*# opponents = game_state\['board'\]\['snakes'\]", new_code, content, flags=re.DOTALL)

with open('/workspace/main.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF
python3 /tmp/edit.py
cat /workspace/main.py
"}

# Welcome to
# __________         __    __  .__                               __
# \______   \_____ _/  |__/  |_|  |   ____   ______ ____ _____  |  | __ ____
#  |    |  _/\__  \\   __\   __\  | _/ __ \ /  ___//    \\__  \ |  |/ // __ \
#  |    |   \ / __ \|  |  |  | |  |_\  ___/ \___ \|   |  \/ __ \|    <\  ___/
#  |________/(______/__|  |__| |____/\_____>______>___|__(______/__|__\\_____>
#
# This file can be a nice home for your Battlesnake logic and helper functions.
#
# To get you started we've included code to prevent your Battlesnake from moving backwards.
# For more info see docs.battlesnake.com

import random
import typing


# info is called when you create your Battlesnake on play.battlesnake.com
# and controls your Battlesnake's appearance
# TIP: If you open your Battlesnake URL in a browser you should see this data
def info() -> typing.Dict:
    print("INFO")

    return {
        "apiversion": "1",
        "author": "",  # TODO: Your Battlesnake Username
        "color": "#888888",  # TODO: Choose color
        "head": "default",  # TODO: Choose head
        "tail": "default",  # TODO: Choose tail
    }


# start is called when your Battlesnake begins a game
def start(game_state: typing.Dict):
    print("GAME START")


# end is called when your Battlesnake finishes a game
def end(game_state: typing.Dict):
    print("GAME OVER\n")


# move is called on every turn and returns your next move
# Valid moves are "up", "down", "left", or "right"
# See https://docs.battlesnake.com/api/example-move for available data
def move(game_state: typing.Dict) -> typing.Dict:

    is_move_safe = {"up": True, "down": True, "left": True, "right": True}

    # We've included code to prevent your Battlesnake from moving backwards
    my_head = game_state["you"]["body"][0]  # Coordinates of your head
    my_neck = game_state["you"]["body"][1]  # Coordinates of your "neck"

    if my_neck["x"] < my_head["x"]:  # Neck is left of head, don't move left
        is_move_safe["left"] = False

    elif my_neck["x"] > my_head["x"]:  # Neck is right of head, don't move right
        is_move_safe["right"] = False

    elif my_neck["y"] < my_head["y"]:  # Neck is below head, don't move down
        is_move_safe["down"] = False

    elif my_neck["y"] > my_head["y"]:  # Neck is above head, don't move up
        is_move_safe["up"] = False


    board_width = game_state['board']['width']
    board_height = game_state['board']['height']
    my_body = game_state['you']['body']
    opponents = game_state['board']['snakes']

    possible_moves = {
        "up": {"x": my_head["x"], "y": my_head["y"] + 1},
        "down": {"x": my_head["x"], "y": my_head["y"] - 1},
        "left": {"x": my_head["x"] - 1, "y": my_head["y"]},
        "right": {"x": my_head["x"] + 1, "y": my_head["y"]}
    }

    for move, next_pos in possible_moves.items():
        if not is_move_safe[move]:
            continue
        
        # Step 1 - Prevent your Battlesnake from moving out of bounds
        if next_pos["x"] < 0 or next_pos["x"] >= board_width or next_pos["y"] < 0 or next_pos["y"] >= board_height:
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue

        # Step 2 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with itself
        if next_pos in my_body[:-1]: # Tail might move, but to be safe we can just check all or all but last. The prompt says "moves into your own body are unsafe".
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue

        # Step 3 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with other Battlesnakes
        collision = False
        for snake in opponents:
            if next_pos in snake['body'][:-1]:
                collision = True
                break
        if collision:
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue


    # Are there any safe moves left?
    safe_moves = []
    for move, isSafe in is_move_safe.items():
        if isSafe:
            safe_moves.append(move)

    if len(safe_moves) == 0:
        print(f"MOVE {game_state['turn']}: No safe moves detected! Moving down")
        return {"move": "down"}

    # Choose a random move from the safe ones
    next_move = random.choice(safe_moves)

    # TODO: Step 4 - Move towards food instead of random, to regain health and survive longer
    # food = game_state['board']['food']

    print(f"MOVE {game_state['turn']}: {next_move}")
    return {"move": next_move}


# Start server when `python main.py` is run
if __name__ == "__main__":
    from server import run_server

    run_server({"info": info, "start": start, "move": move, "end": end})

def move(game_state: typing.Dict) -> typing.Dict:

    is_move_safe = {"up": True, "down": True, "left": True, "right": True}

    # We've included code to prevent your Battlesnake from moving backwards
    my_head = game_state["you"]["body"][0]  # Coordinates of your head
    my_neck = game_state["you"]["body"][1]  # Coordinates of your "neck"

    if my_neck["x"] < my_head["x"]:  # Neck is left of head, don't move left
        is_move_safe["left"] = False

    elif my_neck["x"] > my_head["x"]:  # Neck is right of head, don't move right
        is_move_safe["right"] = False

    elif my_neck["y"] < my_head["y"]:  # Neck is below head, don't move down
        is_move_safe["down"] = False

    elif my_neck["y"] > my_head["y"]:  # Neck is above head, don't move up
        is_move_safe["up"] = False


    board_width = game_state['board']['width']
    board_height = game_state['board']['height']
    my_body = game_state['you']['body']
    opponents = game_state['board']['snakes']

    possible_moves = {
        "up": {"x": my_head["x"], "y": my_head["y"] + 1},
        "down": {"x": my_head["x"], "y": my_head["y"] - 1},
        "left": {"x": my_head["x"] - 1, "y": my_head["y"]},
        "right": {"x": my_head["x"] + 1, "y": my_head["y"]}
    }

    for move, next_pos in possible_moves.items():
        if not is_move_safe[move]:
            continue
        
        # Step 1 - Prevent your Battlesnake from moving out of bounds
        if next_pos["x"] < 0 or next_pos["x"] >= board_width or next_pos["y"] < 0 or next_pos["y"] >= board_height:
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue

        # Step 2 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with itself
        if next_pos in my_body[:-1]: # Tail might move, but to be safe we can just check all or all but last. The prompt says "moves into your own body are unsafe".
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue

        # Step 3 - Prevent your Battlesnake from colliding with other Battlesnakes
        collision = False
        for snake in opponents:
            if next_pos in snake['body'][:-1]:
                collision = True
                break
        if collision:
            is_move_safe[move] = False
            continue


    # Are there any safe moves left?
    safe_moves = []
    for move, isSafe in is_move_safe.items():
        if isSafe:
            safe_moves.append(move)

    if len(safe_moves) == 0:
        print(f"MOVE {game_state['turn']}: No safe moves detected! Moving down")
        return {"move": "down"}

    # Choose a random move from the safe ones
    next_move = random.choice(safe_moves)

    # TODO: Step 4 - Move towards food instead of random, to regain health and survive longer
    # food = game_state['board']['food']

    print(f"MOVE {game_state['turn']}: {next_move}")
    return {"move": next_move}


# Start server when `python main.py` is run
if __name__ == "__main__":
    from server import run_server

    run_server({"info": info, "start": start, "move": move, "end": end})

# Agent Handoff Notes

Implemented the next narrow safety layer in `main.py`:
- Added board-boundary checks to prevent moving off the board.
- Added self-collision checks to prevent moving into our own body (excluding the tail which moves).
- Added opponent-body collision checks to prevent moving into other snakes' bodies (excluding their tails).

The edits are bounded and preserve the existing random safe move logic.
EOF
git status --short && git diff --check
"}
